title: edhoc_kdf: .expect() calls on HKDF operations could panic on edge cases
description: In rust/lichen-oscore/src/edhoc.rs edhoc_kdf(), the code uses .expect() on Hkdf::from_prk() and hk.expand(). While the comments say these are infallible for the given inputs, using .expect() in a cryptographic protocol implementation is risky. A subtle bug in the PRK derivation or an unexpected input could cause a panic. Consider propagating these as EdhocError instead of panicking, especially since the function already returns Result.
